pages.full_text:
distant hills all round. Daffodils (late spring) 3 graves in middle of camp site, house wi. shutters, pond, bridge, Welsh flag, clean loo.
  __April 10th__. Mummy's + Daddy's 81st wedding anniversary.
  Gorgeous sunshine. Relaxed. On the road by 9.15.
  We parked + had coffee + set off with lunch on a beautiful walk at about 10.45 from Torpantau where the Brecon to Merthyr railway emerged from a tunnel. We followed a forestry road down to the river where we crossed to the west bank and walked north parallel to the river until the Lower Neuadd Reservoir.  We crossed the dam after having lunch in a sunny spot and turned north again up the old "Roman Road" to the pass overlooking Brecon.  Eve thought Tonic had walked enough so carried him back from here in the backpack.  It was a marvellous
pages.notes: Page is a continuation from a previous page — the first lines begin mid-sentence about a campsite. The date 'April 10th' is underlined. 'Tonic' appears to be a dog. The year is not stated on this page; the volume covers 2003–2019 and the reference to Mummy's & Daddy's '81st wedding anniversary' provides no absolute year anchor without knowing the wedding year. Positioned roughly mid-volume so a date in the mid-2000s to 2010s is plausible; year set tentatively to null in iso_guess pending corroboration from adjacent pages.

pages.full_text:
walk + we caught some sun.**
  As we neared __Crickhowell__ I telephoned Joan Clarke, now 92, + we went round to see her taking supper.  Briefly, in the hall, we met her niece, Jane, who was on her way out to supper.  Joan seems very happy where she lives Flat 21 Maes y Ffynnon, Beaufort St. 01873-810664.  (campsite £15, expensive + extra charge for showers)

__Sunday April 11th__.
  Another sunny morning.  We ate up half breakfast, walked up to St. Edmund's church where we heard about the re-setting-up of a credit union + then walked down for the other half of breakfast.  Tonic + I also went to see the dog walk by the river.
  10.30. we set off.
  We visited Abbey Dore.  Four young men in one family died in WW2 in the RAF + their 16 year old [?sister] died in 1936, according to one of the [illegible] graves outside.  We had a lunch in the pub carvery where they are polite but the food was pretty dire.
  We called in at Trampley on the way home + had tea in the summer house.
pages.notes: Page number '25' visible in top right corner. 'Crickhowell' is underlined. 'Sunday April 11th' is underlined as a heading. 'Maes y Ffynnon' could also be read as 'Maes y Ffynon' — the diary spelling is followed. The word before 'graves' is struck through (written over) and partially illegible — could be 'bomb' or 'stone'; transcribed as [illegible]. 'Trampley' could alternatively be 'Trumpley' — the initial letter and middle letters are somewhat ambiguous. The year is not given on this page; 'Sunday April 11' was a Sunday in 2004 and 1999 within the volume's range; 2004 used tentatively.
